concept: Breathe / Respire / Draw and Release Breath
description: The fundamental act of drawing air in and releasing it out—the rhythm that sustains life from first cry to final sigh. 'Phira' names both the automatic process and the conscious practice of breath awareness. It is the bridge between voluntary and involuntary, the place where attention meets autonomy.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'phi' /ɸi/, where the bilabial fricative is breath itself—air shaped minimally into sound, the body demonstrating what the word means. The high 'i' draws inward with the inhale. Then 'ra' releases and flows outward, the exhale completing the cycle.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an intransitive verb: 'mi phira' (I breathe). Can be modified for quality: 'mi hisu na phira' (I breathe peacefully). Related to whunei (breath, noun).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Breath awareness is the foundation of meditation practice—always available, always present, the simplest anchor for mindfulness.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Breath was understood as life-force itself, the animating spirit. To breathe consciously connects us to this ancient knowing.
